Title: B2B Automated Invoice Factoring Underwriter
Product Description: A retail bank builds a machine learning scoring engine to evaluate the creditworthiness of small UK businesses applying for fast invoice factoring lines. The model runs automated calculations on past transaction volumes, credit history, and supplier data to make binding credit line approvals or denials.
Expected Role: Provider (Built internally to put into service on their own platform).
Notes: Falls directly under Annex III, Point 5(b): AI systems intended to be used to evaluate the creditworthiness of natural persons or establish their credit score (extended here via small business structures affecting individuals/sole traders).

Title: AML Alert Triage Optimization Engine
Product Description: A high-street bank in the UK purchases an AI model from a US vendor to ingest millions of clearing house events, score them, and dynamically filter out low-level Anti-Money Laundering (AML) transaction alerts before they can reach human analyst desks.
Expected Role: Deployer (Using a third-party commercial AI in a professional capacity).
S2 (Supply Chain): 100% (Purchased from an external provider with vendor terms).
S5 (Integration): 100% (Integrated directly into the core AML monitoring stack).
Notes: Classified under Annex III (Administration of Justice/Essential Infrastructure touchpoints) as it automates systemic security triage impacting access to financial system integrity and individual financial profiling. Excluded from Annex III because it is an AML system.

Title: Engineering Recruitment & CV Ranking Pipeline
Product Description: A London quantitative trading platform designs an AI tool to parse and rank candidate CVs, GitHub commit records, and coding exercise scripts to select the top 2% of applicants for their graduate scheme.
Expected Role: Provider (Built proprietary pipeline for corporate use).
S3 (Autonomy): 80% (Ranks and filters; human picks from the top tier, but low scores are hidden).
Notes: Falls perfectly under Annex III, Point 4(a): AI systems intended to be used for the recruitment or selection of natural persons, notably for advertising vacancies, screening or filtering applications, and evaluating candidates.
